records.ety: [Probably of imitative origin; perhaps influenced by dump, v. t.]

defs: The sound made by the sudden fall or blow of a heavy body, as of a hammer, or the like.
quotes: The distant forge's swinging thump profound.
With heavy thump, a lifeless lump, They dropped down, one by one.

defs: A blow or knock, as with something blunt or heavy; a heavy fall.
quotes: The watchman gave so great a thump at my door, that I awaked at the knock.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Thumped (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Thumping.]

defs: To strike or beat with something thick or heavy, or so as to cause a dull sound.
quotes: These bastard Bretons; whom our hathers Have in their own land beaten, bobbed, and thumped.

defs: To give a thump or thumps; to strike or fall with a heavy blow; to pound.
quotes: A watchman at midnight thumps with his pole.
